ID:Attribute.Definition: The name of a {c:bold}class{/c} that inherits from {c:color-white keyword}System.{/c}{c:color-green keyword}Attribute{/c}.{JumpExt}

ID:ClassDeclaration.Definition: A {c:bold}class{/c} declaration defines a blueprint for creating objects that contain data and behavior. It groups related variables and methods into a single reusable unit.{RefExt}

ID:ConstructorDeclaration.Definition: A {c:bold}constructor{/c} is a special method that runs when a new instance of an object is created. Defining input parameters is {c:bold}optional{/c} but can be helpful in setting up the object's initial state.

ID:ConstructorInvocation.Definition: The name of a type {c:color-green bold}constructor{/c}.{JumpExt}

ID:DelegateDeclaration.Definition: A {c:bold}delegate{/c} declaration defines a type that represents a reference to a method. Delegates allow methods to be passed around and called later.

ID:EnumDeclaration.Definition: An {c:bold}enum{/c} declaration defines a set of named constants. It is commonly used to represent a fixed list of related values.

ID:EnumMemberDeclaration.Definition: An {c:bold}enum member{/c} declaration defines a single named value inside an enum. Each member represents one possible option from the enum.

ID:EventDeclaration.Definition: An {c:bold}event{/c} declaration defines a notification that other code can respond to when something happens. Events are commonly used for signaling changes or actions.

ID:EventFieldDeclaration.Definition: An {c:bold}event field{/c} declaration declares an event that uses a delegate type behind the scenes. It allows objects to subscribe and react when the event is raised.

ID:FieldDeclaration.Definition: A {c:bold}field{/c} declaration defines a variable that belongs to a class or struct. Fields store data that represents the state of an object. It is only accessible from within the type.

ID:FieldReference.Definition: The name of a {c:color-white bold}field{/c} declared within the containing type.

ID:GenericMethodDeclaration.Definition: A {c:bold}generic method{/c} declaration defines a method with one or more type parameters so the same method can work with different data types.

ID:InterfaceDeclaration.Definition: The identifier of an {c:color-blue keyword}interface{/c} declaration. An implementation must be provided by any type that implements this interface. This allows different classes to share the same behavior without sharing implementation.

ID:LambdaParameter.Definition: The name of a {c:color-light-blue bold}lambda parameter{/c} that can be used inside the lambda expression it's declared in.<break/>Its value is supplied each time the lambda expression runs, typically from the current item in a collection or from an argument passed into the expression.

ID:LambdaParameterReference.Definition: The name of the {c:color-light-blue bold}lambda parameter{/c} declared before the lambda operator ({c:code}=>{/c}) in the current lambda expression.

ID:LocalFunctionDeclaration.Definition: The name of a {c:color-yellow bold}local function{/c}. A local function is declared inside another member or local scope and acts like a helper function that is only available within that containing scope.

ID:LocalVariableDeclaration.Definition: A {c:bold}local variable{/c} declaration defines a variable inside a method or block of code. Local variables are only accessible within the scope where they are declared.

ID:LocalVariableReference.Definition: The name of a {c:color-light-blue bold}local varaible{/c} declared above within the containing scope.

ID:MethodDeclaration.Definition: A {c:bold}method{/c} declaration defines a block of code that performs an action or returns a value. Methods are called to run the code they contain.

ID:MethodInvocation.Definition: The name of a {c:color-yellow bold}method{/c} being invoked.{HovExt}

ID:Parameter.Definition: The name of a {c:color-light-blue bold}parameter{/c} that is accessible anywhere within the following method or constructor body.

ID:ParameterReference.Definition: The name of a {c:color-light-blue bold}parameter{/c} defined in the containing method or constructor {c:tt color-light-blue}signature{/c}.

ID:PropertyDeclaration.Definition: A {c:bold}property{/c} declaration defines a member that provides controlled access to data on an object. Properties often include logic for getting or setting values.

ID:PropertyReference.Definition: The name of a {c:bold}property{/c} defined within the containing type.

ID:RecordDeclaration.Definition: A {c:bold}record{/c} declaration defines a reference type designed for storing data. Records provide built-in support for value-based equality and immutability.

ID:RecordStructDeclaration.Definition: A {c:bold}record struct{/c} declaration defines a value type designed for storing data. Record structs combine the efficiency of structs with record-style features.

ID:StructDeclaration.Definition: A {c:bold}struct{/c} declaration defines a value type that groups related data together. Structs are often used for small, lightweight data objects.

ID:GenericType.Definition: A type that must be supplied a {c:bold}generic type argument{/c}. The generic type argument passed in will replace the {c:bold}generic type parameter{/c} in the type definition.
